*** 3 FATAL *** CONFIRMED TORNADO IN THE CITY LIMITS OF MENA MOVING WEST TO EAST FROM HIGHWAY 8 WEST OF TOWN. REPORTS OF HOUSES DAMAGED WITH WINDOWS BLOWN OUT...TREES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES. A PRELIMINARY RATING OF EF3 HAS BEEN ASSIGNED TO THE DAMAGE WITHIN THE CITY OF MENA. FURTHER SURVEYS WILL BE CONDUCTED OVER THE NEXT FEW DAYS TO DETERMINE THE STARTING AND ENDING POINT OF THE TORNADO. THERE WERE THREE FATALITIES. THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF INJURIES IS UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME.

DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO A HUNTING CLUB. THIS DAMAGE WILL BE CONFIRMED FRIDAY AFTER A DAMAGE SURVEY TO DETERMINE IF IT WAS IN FACT A TORNADO.

THE COUNTY JUDGE REPORTED A WIDE PATH OF TREES THAT WERE BLOWN DOWN ACROSS JACKS BAY ROAD. THE COUNTY IS WORKING TO CLEAR THE ROAD. THERE WERE SEVERAL CAMPERS THAT HAVE BEEN UNABLE TO EXIT THE WHITE RIVER NATIONAL WILDLIFE REFUGE DUE TO THE TREES BLOCKING THE ROAD. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED. THE DAMAGE SURVEY WILL BE CONDUCTED ON FRIDAY TO DETERMINE IF IT WAS IN FACT A TORNADO. THIS WAS THE SAME STORM THAT CAUSED THE DAMAGE TO THE HUNTING CLUB OUTSIDE OF TICHNOR.
